title: GPDE Workshop - External doubly stochastic measures and optimal transportation
speaker: Robert McCann

proof_stages.label: Convex-analytic formulation of transport and doubly stochastic measures
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins by reducing questions of optimal transport to linear optimization over compact convex sets of couplings, with extreme points of finite doubly stochastic matrices and infinite doubly stochastic measures as the guiding objects.

proof_stages.label: Acyclic supports as a partial extremality criterion
proof_stages.summary: After explaining why support-based criteria must survive arbitrary measure-preserving rearrangements and why the Douglas-Lindenstrauss functional criterion is not geometrically transparent, the speaker introduces the finite acyclicity criterion and its one-way infinite-dimensional analogue, together with the obstruction to a naive converse.

proof_stages.label: Numbered limb systems and uniqueness of supported couplings
proof_stages.summary: Hestir-Williams numbered limb systems refine acyclicity, and for Borel limbs there is at most one doubly stochastic measure supported on the system, proved by a finite-limb induction and then a probability/Cauchy limiting argument.

proof_stages.label: Topological uniqueness theorem for relative-cost functions
proof_stages.summary: For optimal transport on manifolds, the classical no-critical-points hypothesis forces a one-limb graph solution, while the new result permits relative-cost functions with only one global maximum and one global minimum and obtains uniqueness with support on at most one graph and one antigraph.

proof_stages.label: Duality and contact-set proof of the two-limb theorem
proof_stages.summary: Kantorovich duality places any optimizer on the zero set of c(x,y)-u(x)-v(y), and differentiability of the c-transform plus the global max/min ordering of competing touching curves forces this contact set to be a numbered limb system with one graph and one antigraph, so Hestir-Williams uniqueness applies.

proof_stages.label: Cost-induced symplectic and pseudo-Riemannian structures
proof_stages.summary: On the nondegenerate mixed-Hessian locus of the cost, the speaker defines a symplectic form and a split-signature pseudo-metric on the product manifold and recalls the notions of Lagrangian and spacelike submanifolds.

proof_stages.label: MTW cross-curvature and geometric characterization of optimal maps
proof_stages.summary: Under twist/convexity and nonnegative Ma-Trudinger-Wang cross-curvature hypotheses, smooth optimal couplings live on diffeomorphism graphs, and conversely a diffeomorphism graph that is omega-Lagrangian and h-spacelike is optimal, with the curvature condition interpreted as an invariant and essentially sharp regularity condition.

examples.engagement_reason: he explains why on a compact manifold such as the sphere the no-critical-points hypothesis must fail, since the relevant smooth function has a maximum and minimum
